content:
First, set up coordinates so that line A is the x‐axis and the big circle B has centre \((0,R)\) with \(R=2008\).  A small circle \(C_n\) of radius \(r_n\) has centre \((x_n,r_n)\), is tangent to the x‐axis so \(y\)-coordinate \(=r_n\), and is tangent externally to \(B\), so

\[
(x_n-0)^2+(r_n-R)^2=(r_n+R)^2
\;\Longrightarrow\;
x_n^2=4R\,r_n\;\Longrightarrow\;x_n=2\sqrt{R\,r_n}.
\]

For \(n\ge2\), \(C_n\) is also tangent to \(C_{n-1}\), so
\[
(x_n-x_{n-1})^2+(r_n-r_{n-1})^2=(r_n+r_{n-1})^2
\;\Longrightarrow\;
x_n-x_{n-1}=2\sqrt{r_n\,r_{n-1}}.
\]
But \(x_n=2\sqrt{R\,r_n}\) and \(x_{n-1}=2\sqrt{R\,r_{n-1}}\).  Hence
\[
2\sqrt{R\,r_n}-2\sqrt{R\,r_{n-1}}
 \;=\;2\sqrt{r_n\,r_{n-1}}
\;\Longrightarrow\;
\sqrt{r_n}=\frac{\sqrt R\,\sqrt{r_{n-1}}}{\sqrt R-\sqrt{r_{n-1}}}.
\]
Set \(t_n=\sqrt{r_n}\) and \(S=\sqrt R\).  Then
\[
t_n=\frac{S\,t_{n-1}}{\,S-t_{n-1}\,},\;t_1=1.
\]
It is easy to check by induction that
\[
t_n=\frac{S}{\,S-(n-1)\,},\qquad
r_n=t_n^2=\frac{R}{\bigl(S-(n-1)\bigr)^2}.
\]
We require \(r_n<R\), i.e.\ \(t_n<S\).  Hence
\[
\frac{S}{\,S-(n-1)\,}<S
\;\Longleftrightarrow\;
S-(n-1)>1
\;\Longleftrightarrow\;
n<S.
\]
Since \(S=\sqrt{2008}\approx44.82\), the largest integer \(n< S\) is \(n=44\).  One checks that \(r_{44}<R\) but \(r_{45}>R\), so exactly 44 such circles fit.  

Answer: \(\boxed{44}\).

Answer: \(\boxed{44}\).
